architecture.summary: This is a survey paper on knowledge distillation (KD) of large language models. It does not present a single unified system but rather categorizes and reviews existing KD approaches across three dimensions: (1) KD Algorithms—methods for eliciting knowledge from teacher LLMs (labeling, expansion, curation, feature extraction, feedback, self-knowledge) and injecting it into student models (supervised fine-tuning, divergence/similarity minimization, reinforcement learning, rank optimization); (2) Skill Distillation—techniques for enhancing specific student capabilities (context following, alignment, agent abilities, NLP task specialization, multi-modality); and (3) Verticalization Distillation—domain-specific applications (law, healthcare, finance, science). The general pipeline involves: (i) steering a teacher LLM toward a target skill/domain via instructions, (ii) providing seed knowledge as input, (iii) generating distillation knowledge (QA, explanations, logits, features), and (iv) training the student model with a learning objective to minimize divergence from teacher outputs.

biblio.title: A Survey on Knowledge Distillation of Large Language Models

claims.statement: Knowledge distillation in the LLM era shifts the focus from mere architecture compression to knowledge elicitation and transfer, largely due to the expansive and deep-seated knowledge that LLMs like GPT-4 and Gemini possess.
KD techniques have surged as a means to bridge the performance gap between proprietary LLMs (e.g., GPT-4, Gemini) and open-source LLMs (e.g., LLaMA, Mistral).

Data augmentation (DA) emerges as a prevalent paradigm to achieve knowledge distillation of LLMs, where a small seed of knowledge is used to prompt the LLM to generate more data with respect to a specific skill or domain.
KD retains its fundamental role in compressing LLMs, making them more efficient without significant loss in performance.
The strategy of employing open-source LLMs as teachers for their own self-improvement has emerged as a promising approach, enhancing their capabilities significantly.

Modern knowledge distillation in LLMs emphasizes the transfer of more abstract qualities such as reasoning patterns, preference alignment, and value alignment, in contrast to earlier focus on output replication.
Knowledge distillation in LLMs involves extracting knowledge in forms including question-and-answer (QA) dialogues, narrative explanations, logits, and hidden features from teacher models.
